              Backend:
              none
              
              changes apply on next task
            

            

              

                
Approval rules

                Per-category gates for the internal agent. Applied live and saved to intendant.toml.
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                Per-category gates for the internal agent. Auto: run without asking. Ask: prompt for approval. Deny: block outright. Applied live and saved to intendant.toml [approval].
              

            

            

              

                Thread actions
                

                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                

                

                  Actions dispatch to the live Codex thread. New thread is the only button that takes effect without an active task; typing /fast with no target starts an idle fast Codex session. Goal actions use Codex's experimental persisted goal API. Errors surface as a toast + Activity log entry.
                

              

              

                Sandbox
                

                  
                  
                

                

                  workspace-write blocks listener sockets / pipes and outbound network. Prefer danger-full-access only when a task genuinely needs live app automation (UNO, D-Bus) or arbitrary network.
                

              

              

                Approval policy
                

                  
                  
                

                

                  untrusted: only run known-safe commands without approval. on-request: model asks when it wants to. never: execute without asking (only with an external sandbox).
                

              

              

                Model
                

                  
                  
                

                
Sent as model in thread/start. Empty = let Codex pick its configured default.

                

                  
                  
                

                
Maps to -c model_reasoning_effort=.... Only meaningful for reasoning-capable models; ignored otherwise.

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  Applies to new Intendant-managed Codex sessions. inherit leaves ~/.codex/config.toml and account defaults in charge; normal explicitly clears Fast for these sessions.
                

              

              

                Tools
                

                  
                  
                  off
                

                

                  Enables the Responses API web_search tool (matches codex --search). No per-call approval.
                

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  vanilla keeps upstream/fork Codex behavior. managed enables proactive Intendant context densification, rewind/backout tools, and suppressed Codex auto-compaction for managed long tasks.
                

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  summary keeps compact visualization snapshots. exact persists full raw provider payloads. off disables Context snapshots.
                

              

              

                Sandbox permissions
                

                  
                  
                  off
                

                

                  Only applies when sandbox is workspace-write. Maps to
                  sandbox_workspace_write.network_access. Middle ground between the default and danger-full-access when a task needs outbound HTTP but no listener binds.
                

                

                  
                  
                

                

                  One absolute path per line. Each becomes an entry in
                  sandbox_workspace_write.writable_roots (equivalent to --add-dir). Blank lines and duplicates are dropped. Press Enter-out or click away to save.
                

              

            

            

              

                Model
                

                  
                  
                

                

                  Sent as claude --model. Empty = let the claude CLI pick its configured default. Applies to the next task (the model latches when the process spawns).
                

              

              

                Permission mode
                

                  
                  
                

                

                  default: tools outside the allowed list ask — prompts surface as Intendant approvals. acceptEdits: file edits auto-approve, commands still ask. plan: read-only planning. auto: a classifier auto-approves safe calls, the rest still ask. dontAsk: anything that would ask is denied instead. bypassPermissions: nothing asks — prefer an external sandbox.
                

              

              

                Allowed tools
                

                  
                  
                

                

                  Passed as claude --allowedTools. Listed tools (rule syntax like Bash(cargo test *) works) skip the permission prompt entirely; everything else follows the permission mode above. Press Ctrl/⌘+Enter or click away to save. Applies to the next task.
                

              

            

            

              Pick Codex in Settings → Agent to configure backend-specific knobs.

By Agent And Period

            
Usage split by agent for today, this week, and all time.

          

          

        

        

          

            
Disk Usage

            
Space used by session recordings, frames, turns, and logs.

                Deep Search
                
              

              

                

                  

                    Log Search
                    Scans every matching session log, including full log files. Large histories can take minutes.
                  

                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                

                
Project narrows the scan before it starts; the remaining controls refine matches after the exhaustive scan.

                  Fueled — model credentials active, ready to launch.
                

                

                  No model credentials on this daemon — the internal agent can’t start. External agents (Codex, Claude Code) sign in with their own accounts and still work.
                  
                

                

                  External agent options
                  

                    
These apply to external agents — pick Codex or Claude Code above to enable.

                    

                      

                        
                        

                          
                          
                        

                      

                      
                      
                      
                      
                      
                      
                      
                      
                      
                      
                      
                      

                        Service tier
                        
                      

                    

                    

                  

                

                
                

                  
                  
                

              

            

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
        
      

      

        

          

            
            
            

            
            
            
          

          

            

              

                
                
                
                
              

              

              

            

            

              

              

              

                

                  
                  
                  
                  
                  
                

                

                  
No file open

                  
Choose a file from the browser on the left, or create one with New file. Every write is checked against this target's filesystem grants.

                

              

              

                
                
                
                
                
                
                
              

            

          

        

        

      

        

          

            
Downloads

            
Pull a file from a daemon to this browser.

          

          

            
            
          

          

          

            
            
            
          

          

          

            

          

        

        

          

            
Transfers

            
Progress and history for downloads and uploads.

            

              
              
            

          

          

        

        

          

            
Uploads

            
Send files from this browser to the daemon — staged for the agent or written to a path.

          

          
          

            
            
            
          

          

            
            
          

          
Drop files

          

          

        

      

        

      

    


    
    

      

        

          

            
Observer Display

            
A headless debug screen the agent can draw on; recordable.

          

          
Not active

          

            
            
          

          

            
Display: :

          

        

        

          

            
Browser Workspaces

            
Managed Chrome instances the agent can drive.

          

          

            
            
            
            
          

          

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
        
        
        
        
        
      


      


        
        

          

            

            

            

            

            

              
Fleet

              
Every daemon you can reach from here. Each one still decides locally what you may do.

            

            

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              
Daemons

              
Machines this dashboard can operate. The route is how you reach one; your role there is decided by that daemon.

              

                
              

            

            

            

              Peer messaging & quick controls
              

                
Talk to the agent on a connected peer directly: send messages, delegate tasks, resolve approvals, and open live displays.

                

              

            

            

              Capability routing
              

                

                  Capability-based discovery and task routing across all connected peers. Capabilities are kebab-case
                  (display, computer-use, task-delegation, …) plus custom:<name>.
                  Separate multiple with commas or spaces.
                

                

                  
Find eligible peers

                  

                    
                    
                  

                  

                

                

                  
Route a task

                  

                    
                  

                  

                  
                  

                    
                    Routes to the lexicographically-first peer that satisfies all required capabilities. Cmd/Ctrl+Enter in the textarea also fires.
                  

                  

                

              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              
People & Devices

              
Humans and their browsers, certificates, and Connect accounts — everyone who may operate this daemon. Daemon-to-daemon trust lives under Peers.

            

            

            

            

            

              Join with an org grant
              

                
Paste the signed document an organization issued for this browser's key. This daemon verifies it against its trusted org keys and writes a normal, expiring IAM grant — nothing changes if verification fails. The document is also kept in this browser and presented automatically whenever you connect to a daemon that trusts the org, so you only do this once.

                
                

                  
                  
                

              

            

            

              
Who has access

              
Every user/client principal this daemon knows, with its grants. Draft and revoked grants stay visible for review.

            

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              
Peer daemons

              
Daemon-to-daemon federation over mutual TLS. A peer profile bounds what a remote daemon may do here — it never grants a human's browser anything.

            

            


            

              

                
Link daemons

                

                  
                  
                  
                  
                


                

                  
Ask another daemon to let this one in. The other side approves the request and picks the profile.

                  

                    
                    
                  

                  

                    
                    
                  

                  

                    
                    
                  

                  

                  
                


                

                  
Paste an invite another daemon created for you. It contains everything needed to connect.

                  
                  

                    
                    
                  

                  
                


                

                  
Create an invite that lets another daemon connect to this one.

                  

                    
                    
                  

                  

                    
                    
                  

                  
                  

                    
                    
                  

                  
Inbound grant: contains a client private key.

                


                

                  
Add a daemon you already hold client certificates for.

                  

                    
                    
                    
                  

                  

                    
                  

                  

                    
                  

                  
                  
                

              


              

                
Inbound Peer Access Requests

                

                

                  
                  
                

              

            


            

              
Inbound Peer Grants

              

              

                
                
              

            


            

              
Peer grants

              
Every daemon-to-daemon grant this daemon knows about, in both directions.

            

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

            

              Connection Diagnostics
              

              

              

                
                
              

              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              
Credential vault

              
Provider credentials, end-to-end encrypted under your passkeys and a recovery phrase. The hosted store holds only ciphertext; daemons only ever borrow time-boxed leases.

            

            


            

              
Custody trail

              
Every lease grant, expiry, revocation, and browser-relay change this daemon has seen — who, what, and when. Recorded by the daemon itself, kept on its disk, never sent anywhere.

            

            


            

              
Roles & permissions

              
The exact permission matrix this daemon enforces. Roles bundle permissions; peer profiles live in the daemon-to-daemon domain.

            

            

            


            

              
All grants

              
Every grant with its principal, target, policy, transport, and the reason it exists.

            

            


            

              
Organizations

              
Orgs whose signed grant documents this daemon accepts, each under a local role cap. Trust is a per-daemon decision — use the grant flow's Apply-to to trust an org across the fleet.

            

            


            

              
Model inspector

              
The raw access model this daemon reports: principals, grants, policies, permissions, IAM roles, audit events, and transports.

            

            

            


            

              
Public sharing

              
Nothing is shared publicly. Explicit public or hosted-service shares remain a design target — nothing is exposed unless you grant it.

            

          

        

      

    


    
    

      

        

          
Vault

        

        

          The credential vault lives in Access → Advanced.
        

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
        
        
      


      


        
        

          

            

              

                
API Keys

                
Provider credentials, saved to ~/.config/intendant/.env.

              

              

                
                
                
              

              

                
                
                
              

              

                
                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

          

        


        
        

          

            

              Settings are saved to intendant.toml and take effect on the next task.
            


            

              

                
External Agent

                
Route tasks to an external coding agent instead of the internal loop.

              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            


            

              

                
Computer Use

                
The model and display backend that drive screen control.

              

              
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

              

                
                
              

            


            

              

                
Presence

                
Text and live-voice models for the companion that mediates between you and the worker.

              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            


            

              

                
Transcription

                
Speech-to-text for voice input and call audio.

              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            


            

              

                
Recording

                
Framerate and quality for display recordings.

              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
                screencapture backend caps at 15 fps
              

              

                
                
              

            


            

              

                
Live Audio

                
Live voice sessions with the agent and their idle timeout.

              

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

          

        


        
        
        

          

            

              

                
Session Report

                
A zip of this session's logs (session.jsonl, turn files, daemon stderr/stdout) for sharing with the dev.

              

              
                Download session report
              
            

            

              Connection diagnostics and self-tests live under
              Access → Diagnostics.
            

            

              
Environment Variable Overrides

              

            

            

              No debug overrides active. Set environment variables before launching intendant --web to see them here.

Delegate to a sub-agent

    

      Spawn a supervised sub-agent under this session. The session is notified and
      collects the result itself; the child shows up in Sessions like any other
      sub-agent.
